_comment: Medical Oncology package M0040C for Relapsed Hodgkin's & Non-Hodgkin's Lymphoma
procedure_name: CT for Relapsed Hodgkin Lymphoma - GDP (Gemcitabine, Dexamethasone, Cisplatin or Carboplatin)
min_doctor_qualification: DM (Medical oncology/ Hematology) or equivalent (MD Medicine with experience or Fellowship in Hematology/Medical oncology)
clinical_indications: Hodgkin lymphoma; history of Hodgkin's lymphoma and previously undergoing treatment for the same (2nd line regimen)

preauth.label: Biopsy report documenting the relapse of HL or NHL and subtype
Disease sites – CECT chest +abdomen +Pelvis or PET-CT
Clinical notes with performance status and disease sites including extranodal sites
Viral markers – HbsAg, HCV and HIV
Details of first line treatment, number of cycles, last date of chemotherapy, response

claim.label: Reports of the tests (pathology, radiology, microbiology, hematology, biochemistry, etc.)
Discharge summary of day care department
Documentation of response after 3 to 4 cycles either with PET-CT or CECT (same as done at diagnosis) and clinical notes

ppd_preauth.q: Is the patient suitable for stem cell transplant or not?
Is the patient >70 years old or frail?
Has the patient received this specific regimen earlier?
Does the patient have renal failure (creatinine clearance less than 50 ml/min)? (Note: This is a cisplatin/carboplatin-based therapy)
Does the patient have poor cardiac function (EF<45%)?

cpd_claim.q: Response after 3 to 4 cycles documented with PET-CT or CECT (same as done at diagnosis) and clinical notes?

common_queries: No document to support relapse (biopsy or imaging missing)
Contraindication (e.g., age >70, frail, prior same regimen, renal failure, poor cardiac function) not addressed
Patient not previously undergoing treatment for Hodgkin Lymphoma
First line treatment details are missing or incomplete
Response to previous treatment not documented

additional_information.special_empanelment: Essential Medical Personnel: Qualified Medical Oncologist/ Hematologist – for chemotherapy; Radiation Oncologist- for radiation. Essential Medical Infrastructure: Chemotherapy unit/ Ward/ Day care/ Injection room/ LINEAC.
clinical_key_pointers: Relapse usually occurs within first 2 years following initial treatment.
Mostly in patients with advanced disease and less often in early stage disease.
Treatment mostly comprises of salvage therapy (high dose therapy), autologous stem cell transplantation & Involved site Radiation Therapy (ISRT).
Relapse categories for diffuse large B cell lymphoma include: (1) those relapsing after complete remission, (2) partial responders with persistent disease, and (3) refractory patients.
All packages are once in a life-time.
Protocols for low grade NHL (second line and subsequent lines) are listed in NHL template.
additional_information.post_op_care: Follow-up care: OPD visits, blood tests, imaging if required, supportive care medications.
additional_information.referral_criteria: Decide referral of cases to the appropriate level of care (as required) for treatment of patients under PMJAY and selection of corresponding Health Benefit Package.
contraindications: If patient has received same regimen earlier
Renal failure (creatinine clearance less than 50 ml/min) – No cisplatin based therapy
